title: Soy candle — magnolia and peony
subtitle: A spring floral soy wax container candle in a glass jar, scented with magnolia and peony fragrance oil
excerpt: Magnolia and peony fragrance oil in a soy wax container candle gives a light, powdery floral scent suited to living rooms and bedrooms. Soy wax burns cleanly and holds floral fragrances well at a 7 to 8% load. This recipe fills a 200 ml glass jar and produces a candle that burns for around 30 hours.
sourceNotes: Soy wax container candle method: Bramble Berry candle-making guide (2023). Fragrance load for soy wax: 6 to 10% of wax weight; 7 to 8% is the practical sweet spot for floral fragrance oils. Wick sizing: CDN-12 or ECO-6 for a 65 mm jar diameter. Pour temperature: 55 to 60°C to minimise sinkholes and improve fragrance retention. Cure time: 3 to 5 days before lighting.

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 candle (200 ml glass jar, ~150 g finished wax)
recipe.batchNotes: Scale the wax and fragrance weights proportionally for multiple jars. Wick each jar individually.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make at least 3 days before the first burn; soy wax candles improve with a longer cure and should not be lit the same day they are poured.
recipe.temperatureNote: Pour at 55 to 60°C. Above 65°C causes sinkholes; below 50°C the wax sets before the fragrance is distributed.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Candles keep two years if stored away from direct sunlight. Fragrance fades after around two years; the wax itself is stable indefinitely. Discard if the wick mushrooms badly or if the candle tunnels on every burn.

glossaryTerms.definition: The percentage of fragrance oil relative to the wax weight. Soy wax container candles hold 6 to 10%; above this the oil pools at the surface rather than binding with the wax.
The first burn of a new candle. The melt pool should reach the edge of the jar before the candle is extinguished. This sets the candle's burn pattern; subsequent burns follow the same depth.

When a candle burns down through the centre without melting the wax at the edges, leaving a tunnel of unburned wax. Caused by an undersized wick or by extinguishing the candle before the first melt pool reaches the jar edge.

content.text: Magnolia and peony fragrance oil produces a clean, powdery floral scent. Soy wax holds floral fragrances well and burns cleanly in a container. The
here is 7% of wax weight. Pour temperature is 55 to 60°C to avoid sinkholes. The candle needs 3 days to cure before the first light. One 200 ml glass jar.

Melt wax on a low heat over a simmering-water bath, not direct on the hob. Never leave melting wax unattended. Wax above its flash point catches like an oil fire; water makes it worse. Keep a kitchen-fire blanket within reach.

items.prepNote: Container-grade soy wax, not pillar grade
7% of wax weight; add at 55°C, below the flash point of most fragrance oils
Pre-tabbed cotton wick, CDN-12 or ECO-6 for a 65 mm jar diameter

content.text: Place the pre-tabbed wick centrally in the jar. Hold it in place with the wick-centring bar resting across the jar rim. Ensure the wick base is flat on the jar floor before pouring.
Weigh the soy wax into the candle pouring pitcher. Melt over a simmering-water bath on low heat, stirring occasionally. Heat to 65°C on the probe thermometer, then remove from heat and allow to cool.

When the wax cools to 55 to 60°C, add the fragrance oil and stir for 2 minutes to distribute evenly. Pour slowly into the jar, leaving 10 mm of headspace at the top. Tap the jar gently on the work surface once to release any air bubbles.
Leave the candle undisturbed at room temperature for 3 to 5 days. Do not move or tilt the jar while the wax sets. Trim the wick to 5 mm before the first burn.
. Burn for long enough that the melt pool reaches the edge of the jar, about 3 to 4 hours for a 65 mm jar. Extinguishing early causes
on subsequent burns. Subsequent burns: trim the wick to 5 mm each time and burn for no longer than 4 hours in a single session.
Two years away from direct sunlight. The fragrance fades after around two years; the wax is stable indefinitely. Store with a lid or cover to keep dust off the wax surface.
